learningObjectives.title: Understand freight booking
learningObjectives.description: Explain booking as capacity reservation for long-distance and international shipments.

learningObjectives.title: Understand booking lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Describe booking flow from planning through confirmation and execution handoff.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate booking and order
learningObjectives.description: Clarify reservation document versus execution document roles.

sections.content: A Freight Booking (FB) is the execution document used primarily for long-distance and international transportation, especially Ocean and Air Freight. It reserves transportation capacity with a carrier before the physical shipment is executed. Unlike a Freight Order, which is commonly used for road transportation, a Freight Booking secures transportation space on vessels, aircraft or trains.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Freight Booking reserves transportation capacity, while Freight Order manages the execution of transportation.

A company exports machinery from Chennai to Hamburg using ocean freight. SAP TM creates a Freight Booking to reserve container space on a shipping vessel. After the carrier confirms the booking, Freight Orders are created for pre-carriage and on-carriage transportation.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Create Freight Bookings as early as possible for ocean and air shipments because transportation capacity may be limited during peak seasons.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An electronics exporter uses SAP TM Freight Bookings to reserve container slots with multiple ocean carriers. Early booking reduced shipment delays during peak export seasons by more than 25%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: Freight Booking reserves transportation capacity, while Freight Order executes transportation. This distinction is frequently tested in SAP TM certification exams.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not use Freight Booking for every shipment. It is primarily intended for long-distance, multimodal, ocean and air transportation scenarios.

items: It is mainly used for Ocean, Air and Rail transportation.
Carrier confirmation is an important part of the booking process.
Freight Booking works together with Freight Orders in multimodal logistics.
It improves international transportation planning and execution.
